doc_title: ARVideo: Autoregressive Pretraining for Self-Supervised Video Representation Learning

items.fact: Vaswani et al. [41] introduced the transformer architecture
items.evidence_span: transformer architecture, as introduced in Vaswani et al. [41]

items.fact: VideoMAE [39, 13] introduces mask autoencoder for self-supervised video representation learning
items.evidence_span: VideoMAE [39, 13] introduces mask autoencoder [18] for self-supervised video video representation learning

Video representation learning has witnessed significant exploration, historically driven by supervised learning methods [40, 43, 37, 4, 26]... SAIM [32] adopts an encoder to autoregressively learn contextual information like a standard vision transformer (ViT)...
We adopt the ViT [9, 39] as the encoder.
We adopt the ViT [9, 39] as the encoder.
Our model utilizes a strategically designed attention mask as in previous work [6, 34] to enable efficient autoregressive prediction in a parallel computation framework.
Our model utilizes a strategically designed attention mask as in previous work [6, 34] to enable efficient autoregressive prediction in a parallel computation framework.
With patch embeddings in ViT, videos can be patchified into nonoverlapping cubes [39, 4, 44, 26] of size $P_{T} \times P_{W} \times P_{H}$.
With patch embeddings in ViT, videos can be patchified into nonoverlapping cubes [39, 4, 44, 26] of size $P_{T} \times P_{W} \times P_{H}$.
With patch embeddings in ViT, videos can be patchified into nonoverlapping cubes [39, 4, 44, 26] of size $P_{T} \times P_{W} \times P_{H}$.
With patch embeddings in ViT, videos can be patchified into nonoverlapping cubes [39, 4, 44, 26] of size $P_{T} \times P_{W} \times P_{H}$.
